 {"id":"cass_memory_system-0htq","title":"[MCP:Future] MCP server architecture design","description":"Run cass-memory as MCP server for direct agent integration:
- No CLI parsing overhead
- Native tool calls from agents
- Real-time communication

Benefits:
- Faster than CLI invocation
- Richer error handling
- Streaming support for long operations

This is a future enhancement (P4 priority) that will enable agents to interact with cass-memory more efficiently through the Model Context Protocol.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:27.530409-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:15:03.001933-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-0l6","title":"[Cass] cassView - Get specific line from session","description":"Implement cassView() to get a specific line from a session.

## Signature
```typescript
async function cassView(
  sessionPath: string, 
  lineNumber: number,
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<string | null>
```

## CLI Command
`cass view {sessionPath} -n {lineNumber} --json`

## Implementation
- Use execFile() to run view command
- Return exact line content
- Return null on error

## Error Handling
- Return null if session not found
- Return null if line number out of range
- No retries needed

## Parameters
- sessionPath: Full path to session file
- lineNumber: Specific line to retrieve (1-indexed)

## Output
Returns just the content of the specified line without line number prefix.

## Usage
Precise line retrieval for:
- Following up on search hits
- Fetching specific context
- Validation and verification

```typescript
const line = await cassView("/path/to/session.txt", 42);
if (line) {
  console.log("Line 42:", line);
}
```

## vs cassExpand
- cassView: Single line only, exact content
- cassExpand: Context window around line, includes line numbers

## Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 4: Session or line not found","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:02.815833-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.182043-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:43:59.085236-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-0q0","title":"[LLM] PROMPTS.reflector - Delta extraction prompt template","description":"Full prompt template for delta extraction with variables:
{existingBullets}, {status}, {accomplishments}, {decisions}, {challenges}, {preferences}, {keyLearnings}, {cassHistory}, {iterationNote}

Key delta types:
1. ADD: New insight not covered by existing bullets - MUST be specific
2. HELPFUL: Existing bullet proved useful
3. HARMFUL: Following existing bullet caused problems
4. REPLACE: Existing bullet needs updated wording
5. DEPRECATE: Existing bullet is outdated/wrong

Guidelines emphasized:
- Only genuinely reusable insights
- Consider: Would this help a DIFFERENT agent?
- Max 20 deltas per reflection
- Be specific with file names, function names, error messages
- Avoid vague/generic rules

Temperature: 0.5 (slightly higher for creativity)
Used in runReflector function.

Iteration note: On subsequent iterations, remind LLM to focus on novel insights not already proposed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:52.858965-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:53:18.233462-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:49:30.145923-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-0sv","title":"[Generator] checkDeprecatedPatterns - Warn about deprecated usage","description":"Check if deprecated patterns appear in historical context and generate warnings.

SIGNATURE:
function checkDeprecatedPatterns(history: CassHit[], playbook: Playbook): string[]

PURPOSE:
Alert the agent when historical context shows usage of patterns that have been marked as deprecated in the playbook. Helps prevent repeating outdated approaches.

FLOW:
1. Get all deprecatedPatterns from playbook
2. For each deprecated pattern:
   a. Check if pattern.oldPattern appears in any history snippet
   b. If found, generate warning string using pattern.replacement
3. Return array of warning strings

DEPRECATED PATTERN STRUCTURE:
{
  oldPattern: string,        // What to look for (regex or literal)
  replacement: string,       // What to use instead
  reason?: string,          // Why it was deprecated
  deprecatedAt: Date        // When it was deprecated
}

EXAMPLE:
Playbook has:
  { oldPattern: 'AuthHandler', replacement: 'AuthService', reason: 'Refactored to service pattern' }

History contains:
  'Using AuthHandler.authenticate() for login flow'

Output:
  ['AuthHandler class was deprecated - use AuthService instead (Reason: Refactored to service pattern)']

WARNING FORMAT:
'{oldPattern} was deprecated - use {replacement} instead'
If reason exists: append '(Reason: {reason})'

MATCHING LOGIC:
- Case-sensitive substring match by default
- Support regex if pattern starts with '/' (optional enhancement)
- Match against snippet content only (not metadata)

USE CASE:
Prevents agent from learning from old sessions that use deprecated approaches, even if those sessions were marked helpful at the time.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Return empty array if no deprecatedPatterns defined
- Return empty array if history is empty
- Multiple warnings possible (one per deprecated pattern found)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.536656-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:15:25.973645-05:00"}

DEPRECATED PATTERN STRUCTURE:

EXAMPLE:
Playbook has:

History contains:

Output:

WARNING FORMAT:

If reason exists: append '(Reason: {reason})'

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-0z1y","title":"[CLI:stats] renderStatsUI - Pretty terminal output","description":"Implements the renderStatsUI function that creates beautiful terminal output for playbook statistics.

FUNCTION SIGNATURE:
```typescript
function renderStatsUI(stats: PlaybookStats): string
```

OUTPUT FORMAT:
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│              CASS-MEMORY PLAYBOOK HEALTH            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Total Bullets: 156 (Global: 89, Workspace: 67)      │
│                                                     │
│ BY MATURITY:                                        │
│   ● Candidate:   23 (14.7%)  ░░░░░                  │
│   ● Established: 87 (55.8%)  ████████████████       │
│   ● Proven:      41 (26.3%)  ████████               │
│   ● Deprecated:   5 ( 3.2%)  █                      │
│                                                     │
│ BY TYPE:                                            │
│   ● Rules:         142 (91.0%)                      │
│   ● Anti-Patterns:  14 ( 9.0%)                      │
│                                                     │
│ SCORE DISTRIBUTION:                                 │
│   🌟 Excellent (8+):  28 (17.9%)                    │
│   ✓  Good (4-7):      94 (60.3%)                    │
│   –  Neutral (0-3):   29 (18.6%)                    │
│   ⚠  At Risk (<0):     5 ( 3.2%)                    │
│                                                     │
│ TOP PERFORMERS:                                     │
│   1. [12.4] Use React.memo for expensive renders   │
│   2. [11.8] Run migrations in transaction          │
│   3. [10.2] Validate input at API boundary         │
│                                                     │
│ ⚠  AT RISK (5):                                     │
│   • [-2.1] abc-123: Cache without expiry           │
│   • [-1.5] def-456: Skip error handling            │
│                                                     │
│ 📊 STALE BULLETS (12): No feedback in 90+ days     │
│ 🔗 MERGE CANDIDATES (3): High similarity detected  │
│                                                     │
│ 💡 RECOMMENDATIONS:                                 │
│   • Review at-risk bullets: cass-memory show <id>  │
│   • Consider merging similar rules                 │
│   • Clean up stale bullets                         │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS:

1. BOX DRAWING:
   - Use Unicode box characters (╭╮╰╯├┤│─)
   - Dynamic width based on terminal size
   - Proper padding and alignment

2. COLOR CODING (using chalk):
   - Green: Good metrics, top performers
   - Yellow: Neutral, warnings
   - Red: At risk, negative scores
   - Blue: Headers, section titles
   - Gray: Secondary information

3. VISUAL ELEMENTS:
   - Bar charts using █░ characters
   - Percentage display with proper formatting
   - Icons/emoji for quick scanning
   - Proper number formatting (1,234 vs 1234)

4. RESPONSIVE LAYOUT:
   - Detect terminal width
   - Truncate long bullet previews with ellipsis
   - Adjust bar chart length to fit
   - Collapse sections if terminal too narrow

5. INFORMATION HIERARCHY:
   - Most important metrics at top
   - Actionable items highlighted
   - Supporting details below
   - Recommendations at bottom

CONTENT PRIORITIZATION:
- Show top 3 performers (not all 10)
- Show all at-risk bullets (critical)
- Summarize stale/merge counts (details via other commands)
- Include next-action recommendations

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Works in non-color terminals (fallback to ASCII)
- Readable without emoji support
- Screen reader friendly (avoid pure visual elements)

DEPENDENCIES:
- chalk (colors)
- terminal-size (width detection)
- String utilities (truncate, pad, align)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Output fits in standard 80-column terminal
- Colors enhance readability without being essential
- All key metrics visible at a glance
- Actionable insights clearly highlighted
- Professional appearance suitable for demos","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:54:03.844591-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:54:03.844591-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-14xw","title":"[Docs] CONTRIBUTING.md - Contribution guidelines","description":"Create CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines for developers.

Sections:
1. Development Setup:
   - Prerequisites: Node.js 18+, Bun, Git
   - Clone repository
   - Install dependencies: bun install
   - Run in development: bun run dev
   
2. Running Tests:
   - Unit tests: bun test
   - Integration tests: bun test:integration
   - Coverage: bun test --coverage
   
3. Code Style:
   - TypeScript strict mode enabled
   - Prettier for formatting
   - ESLint for linting
   - Run: bun run lint && bun run format
   
4. PR Process:
   - Fork repository
   - Create feature branch
   - Write tests for new features
   - Ensure all tests pass
   - Update documentation
   - Submit PR with clear description
   
5. Issue Templates:
   - Bug reports
   - Feature requests
   - Documentation improvements
   
6. Architecture Overview:
   - Brief explanation for contributors
   - Link to detailed architecture docs

Code of Conduct: Be respectful and constructive","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:46.118799-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:10:47.883612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:09:52.422736-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-1ce","title":"[Playbook] getBulletsByCategory - Filter bullets by category","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function getBulletsByCategory(
  playbook: Playbook,
  category: string,
  includeDeprecated?: boolean
): PlaybookBullet[]
```

## Purpose
Filter bullets by category with optional inclusion of deprecated bullets. Returns sorted array by effective score.

## Algorithm
1. Filter playbook.bullets by category match
2. If includeDeprecated is false (default):
   - Filter out bullets with state === "retired"
   - Filter out bullets with maturity === "deprecated"
3. Calculate effective score for each bullet
4. Sort by effective score (descending)
5. Return sorted array

## Category Matching
- **Exact match**: category === bullet.category
- **Case-insensitive**: Normalize to lowercase
- **Common categories**: "testing", "security", "performance", "code-quality", "documentation"

## Effective Score Sorting
Priority order:
1. Highest score first (most helpful)
2. Recent validation (newer lastValidatedAt)
3. Maturity level (proven > established > candidate)

## includeDeprecated Parameter
```typescript
// Active bullets only (default)
const testing = getBulletsByCategory(playbook, "testing");

// Include deprecated for historical analysis
const allTesting = getBulletsByCategory(playbook, "testing", true);
```

## Usage Scenarios
1. **Context generation**: Get top N rules for category
2. **Category stats**: Count bullets per category
3. **Markdown export**: Group bullets by category
4. **Search**: Find rules in specific domain

## Implementation Notes
- Case-insensitive category matching
- Sort by effective score for relevance
- Default excludes deprecated (most common use)
- Return empty array if no matches

## Return Value
```typescript
PlaybookBullet[]  // Sorted by effective score DESC
```

## Dependencies
- calculateEffectiveScore() - for sorting
- getActiveBullets() - similar filtering

## Related Functions
- getActiveBullets() - all active bullets
- exportToMarkdown() - uses this for grouping
- findMergeCandidates() - groups by category first","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:10.558681-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:24.45406-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-e21","title":"[Security] Sanitization application points documentation","description":"Document WHERE to apply the sanitize() function throughout the codebase.

Application points (in order of data flow):
1. After cassExport, BEFORE passing to LLM (in diary generation)
   - Raw session content from CASS is untrusted
   - Must sanitize before LLM sees it

2. Before writing DiaryEntry to disk
   - Prevent secrets from persisting in diary files
   - Apply sanitize() to entry.content

3. Before storing content in PlaybookBullet
   - Sanitize before creating bullet objects
   - Apply to bullet.content and bullet.evidence

4. In context output (should already be sanitized if stored sanitized)
   - Defense in depth: verify sanitization at output time
   - Shouldn't find anything if earlier stages worked

GOLDEN RULE: NEVER pass unsanitized session content to LLM or disk

Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Sanitize in diary generation flow
- [ ] Sanitize before DiaryEntry persistence
- [ ] Sanitize before PlaybookBullet creation
- [ ] Add verification at context output

Priority: 0 (Security Critical - P12)","notes":"Added sanitization application points under README Security/Secret Sanitization; communicated to agents. No sanitize.ts changes to avoid conflict with WhiteCat reservation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:49.167852-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.200679-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:45:48.046117-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-e8x","title":"[Playbook] loadPlaybook - Load and validate playbook from YAML","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
async function loadPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>
```

## Purpose
Load and validate playbook from YAML file. Handles missing files, corruption, and schema validation with automatic recovery.

## Algorithm
1. Expand playbookPath from config
2. Check if file exists
3. If not exists: create empty playbook and return
4. Read file content
5. If empty: create new playbook and return
6. Parse YAML content
7. Normalize keys (snake_case → camelCase for compatibility)
8. Validate with PlaybookSchema.safeParse()
9. If invalid: backup to .backup.{timestamp} and create new
10. Return validated playbook

## Error Handling
- **ENOENT**: File doesn't exist → create empty
- **EACCES**: Permission denied → throw error
- **Parse errors**: YAML syntax errors → backup and recreate
- **Validation errors**: Schema mismatch → backup and recreate

## Backup Strategy
When corruption detected:
- Backup to: `{playbookPath}.backup.{timestamp}`
- Log warning with backup location
- Create fresh empty playbook
- Never lose user data

## Implementation Notes
- Use fs.promises for async file operations
- Use yaml.parse() for YAML parsing
- Normalize keys for backward compatibility
- Always validate against current schema version

## Dependencies
- fs/promises
- yaml library
- PlaybookSchema (Zod schema)
- createEmptyPlaybook()

## Related Functions
- createEmptyPlaybook() - creates new structure
- savePlaybook() - writes loaded playbook back","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made. Released reservation; feel free to take over.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:45.760512-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:58:10.124214-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:57:52.629716-05:00"}

## Current Status
- Typecheck shows 13 errors (down from 27+)
- Main conflict: External linter/sync process reverts schema changes

## Core Issue
The linter removes `helpfulEvents`/`harmfulEvents` arrays from PlaybookBulletSchema, but code (mark.ts, curate.ts, scoring.ts) requires them.

## Options
1. Keep arrays: Update schema (being reverted by linter)
2. Remove arrays: Refactor all code to use feedbackEvents.filter()

## Fixes Applied (may be reverted)
- types.ts: Added helpfulEvents/harmfulEvents arrays
- playbook.ts: loadPlaybook now accepts Config|string
- sanitize.ts: SanitizationConfig accepts string[] extraPatterns

## Remaining Errors
- config.ts: auditLog field missing in sanitization default
- types.ts: BulletMaturitySchema should be BulletMaturityEnum
- validate.ts: supportingEvidence/contradictingEvidence missing

## Next Steps
Need project-level decision on helpfulEvents/harmfulEvents architecture before proceeding.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-07T18:57:28.306163-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:20:01.02561-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-efd","title":"[Types] PlaybookBullet Schema Part 1 - Identity and Scope","description":"## Overview
Define identity and scope fields for PlaybookBullet schema. These fields establish bullet uniqueness and applicability hierarchy.

## Identity Field

### id: string
- Format: 'b-{timestamp36}-{random}'
- Example: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8'
- Generation: timestamp in base36 + 4-char random suffix
- Purpose: Globally unique, sortable identifier
- Immutable: Never changes after creation

## Scope Hierarchy (5 Levels)

### scope: enum
Type: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

Hierarchy (broadest to narrowest):

1. global - Universal rules (e.g., 'Write clear commit messages')
   - Applies: Always
   - scopeKey: Not used
   - workspace: Not used

2. workspace - Repository-specific rules
   - Applies: When in matching workspace directory
   - scopeKey: Not used (workspace field holds path)
   - workspace: '/path/to/project'
   - Example: 'Use AuthService from @/lib/auth in this project'

3. language - Language-specific rules
   - Applies: When working in files of that language
   - scopeKey: Language identifier (e.g., 'typescript', 'python', 'rust')
   - workspace: Optional (can be workspace + language)
   - Example: 'For TypeScript, prefer interfaces over types for objects'

4. framework - Framework/library-specific rules
   - Applies: When project uses that framework
   - scopeKey: Framework identifier (e.g., 'nextjs', 'react', 'django')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For Next.js, use app router not pages router'

5. task - Task-type specific rules
   - Applies: When performing that category of work
   - scopeKey: Task category (e.g., 'authentication', 'testing', 'api-design')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For authentication tasks, validate JWT expiration'

## Scope Filtering Logic
When retrieving bullets for context:
1. Include all 'global' scope bullets
2. If in workspace, include matching 'workspace' bullets
3. If file language detected, include matching 'language' bullets
4. If framework detected, include matching 'framework' bullets
5. If task category inferred, include matching 'task' bullets

Multiple scopes can apply simultaneously (e.g., workspace + language + task).

## Fields

### Required
- id: string - Unique identifier
- scope: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

### Optional
- scopeKey: string | undefined
  - Used for: language, framework, task scopes
  - Not used for: global, workspace scopes
  - Examples: 'typescript', 'react', 'testing'

- workspace: string | undefined
  - Used for: workspace scope (required)
  - Optional for: other scopes (adds workspace constraint)
  - Value: Absolute path to workspace root
  - Example: '/Users/dev/projects/my-app'

## Examples

Global Rule: { id: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8', scope: 'global', content: 'Write descriptive commit messages' }

Workspace Rule: { id: 'b-m8n3p5q7-b4g9', scope: 'workspace', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/auth-service', content: 'Use TokenValidator from @/lib/auth for all token checks' }

Language Rule: { id: 'b-n9o4q6r8-c5h0', scope: 'language', scopeKey: 'typescript', content: 'Prefer const assertions over enums for string literals' }

Framework Rule: { id: 'b-o0p5r7s9-d6i1', scope: 'framework', scopeKey: 'nextjs', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/web-app', content: 'Use server actions in app directory, not API routes' }

Task Rule: { id: 'b-p1q6s8t0-e7j2', scope: 'task', scopeKey: 'testing', content: 'Use vitest for unit tests, playwright for e2e' }

## Acceptance Criteria
- id field with format specification
- scope enum with 5 values
- scopeKey optional field
- workspace optional field
- ID generation utility function
- Scope filtering logic implemented
- Examples for all 5 scope types
- JSDoc explaining hierarchy","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.006278-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:45.47468-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ehaq","title":"[CLI:stats] computeFullStats - Calculate all metrics","description":"Implements the computeFullStats function that calculates comprehensive playbook metrics.

FUNCTION SIGNATURE:
```typescript
interface PlaybookStats {
  total: number;
  byScope: { global: number; workspace: number };
  byMaturity: { 
    candidate: number; 
    established: number; 
    proven: number; 
    deprecated: number 
  };
  byType: { rule: number; antiPattern: number };
  scoreDistribution: { 
    excellent: number;    // score >= 8
    good: number;         // score 4-7
    neutral: number;      // score 0-3
    atRisk: number;       // score < 0
  };
  topPerformers: { 
    id: string; 
    score: number; 
    preview: string 
  }[];
  atRisk: { 
    id: string; 
    score: number; 
    reason: string 
  }[];
  stale: { 
    id: string; 
    daysSinceLastFeedback: number 
  }[];
  mergeCandidates: { 
    ids: string[];        // Array of 2+ similar bullet IDs
    similarity: number;   // Semantic similarity score
  }[];
}

function computeFullStats(playbook: Playbook): PlaybookStats
```

COMPUTATION LOGIC:

1. BASIC COUNTS:
   - Iterate all bullets, count by scope/maturity/type
   - Skip deprecated unless explicitly included

2. SCORE DISTRIBUTION:
   - Calculate effective score (with decay) for each bullet
   - Bucket into excellent/good/neutral/atRisk
   - Use consistent thresholds across system

3. TOP PERFORMERS:
   - Sort by effective score descending
   - Take top 10
   - Generate preview (first 60 chars of content)

4. AT RISK BULLETS:
   - Filter bullets with score < 0
   - Include reason: "Negative feedback" or "Declining effectiveness"
   - Sort by score ascending (worst first)

5. STALE BULLETS:
   - Calculate days since last helpful/harmful feedback
   - Filter bullets with >90 days (configurable threshold)
   - Include only active bullets (not deprecated)

6. MERGE CANDIDATES:
   - Compute pairwise semantic similarity (if embeddings available)
   - Find pairs/groups with similarity > 0.90
   - Exclude already-merged or deprecated bullets
   - Limit to top 5 groups

PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS:
- Cache embeddings for similarity computation
- Use single pass for multiple aggregations
- Lazy compute merge candidates (expensive)
- Parallel score calculations for large playbooks

EDGE CASES:
- Empty playbook → return zeros
- No embeddings → skip merge candidates
- All bullets deprecated → show warning
- No feedback data → mark all as stale

DEPENDENCIES:
- Playbook data structure
- Score calculation utilities
- Embedding similarity (optional)
- Date/time utilities for staleness

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- All metrics accurate and consistent
- Performance <500ms for 1000 bullets
- Handles edge cases gracefully
- Stats match reality when spot-checked","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:53:34.560049-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:53:34.560049-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ekh","title":"[Types] Validation Types - Scientific Validation Results","description":"## Overview
Define types for scientific validation system - results from evidence-based rule validation before adding to playbook.

## Purpose
Scientific validation prevents low-quality rules from entering the playbook by:
1. Searching historical sessions for supporting evidence
2. Analyzing success/failure patterns
3. Providing confidence scores
4. Suggesting state (active vs draft)
5. Optionally refining rule text

## ValidationResult (Simple)

Basic validation outcome for quick checks.

Fields:
- valid: boolean (pass or fail)
- reason: string (explanation of verdict)
- refinedContent: string | undefined (optional improved rule text)
- confidence: number | undefined (0-1 confidence score)

Usage: Quick validation checks, preliminary filtering

Example:
{ valid: true, reason: 'Found 5 supporting sessions with consistent pattern', confidence: 0.87 }

{ valid: false, reason: 'No evidence found in recent sessions', confidence: 0.12 }

## ValidationReport (Comprehensive)

Detailed validation analysis with evidence and recommendations.

Fields:

proposedRule: string
- The rule being validated
- Example: 'Use TokenValidator for JWT validation'

verdict: 'ACCEPT' | 'REJECT' | 'ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION'
- ACCEPT: Strong evidence, add to playbook as active
- REJECT: Insufficient/contradictory evidence, do not add
- ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION: Some evidence, add as draft or candidate

confidence: number
- Range: 0-1
- Based on: evidence quality, quantity, recency
- Example: 0.85 (high confidence)

evidence: Evidence[]
- Array of supporting/contradicting evidence
- Structure: { sessionPath: string, snippet: string, type: 'supporting' | 'contradicting', relevance: number }
- Example: [{ sessionPath: '/cass/2025-12-07.jsonl', snippet: 'Used TokenValidator successfully', type: 'supporting', relevance: 0.92 }]

reason: string
- Detailed explanation of verdict
- Includes: evidence summary, patterns observed, concerns
- Example: 'Found 8 supporting sessions over 30 days showing consistent use of TokenValidator for JWT validation. No contradicting patterns. Recommend adding as active rule.'

refinedRule: string | undefined
- Optional improved version of proposed rule
- Enhanced with: specificity, context, examples
- Example: 'Use TokenValidator from @/lib/auth for all JWT validation to prevent authentication bypass'

## Evidence Structure

Supporting evidence for validation.

Fields:
- sessionPath: string (path to session file)
- snippet: string (relevant excerpt from session)
- type: 'supporting' | 'contradicting'
- relevance: number (0-1, how relevant this evidence is)
- timestamp: string | undefined (when session occurred)
- agent: string | undefined (which agent)

## EvidenceGateResult

Result from pre-LLM evidence gate check.

Purpose: Fast filtering before expensive LLM validation

Fields:

passed: boolean
- True if enough evidence to proceed to LLM validation
- False if insufficient evidence (skip LLM, save cost)

reason: string
- Explanation of gate decision
- Example: 'Found 8 sessions with 6 successes, 1 failure - sufficient for validation'

suggestedState: 'draft' | 'active'
- Recommended initial state based on evidence
- draft: Low evidence (1-2 sessions)
- active: Strong evidence (5+ sessions)

sessionCount: number
- Total sessions found matching proposed rule
- Example: 8

successCount: number
- Sessions with positive outcomes
- Example: 6

failureCount: number
- Sessions with negative outcomes
- Example: 1

Example:
{ passed: true, reason: '8 sessions found (6 success, 1 failure, 1 mixed)', suggestedState: 'active', sessionCount: 8, successCount: 6, failureCount: 1 }

{ passed: false, reason: 'Only 1 session found - insufficient evidence', suggestedState: 'draft', sessionCount: 1, successCount: 1, failureCount: 0 }

## Validation Workflow

1. Evidence Gate: Run fast pre-check (EvidenceGateResult)
2. Gate Decision:
   - If passed: Proceed to LLM validation
   - If not passed: Either reject or add as draft (configurable)
3. LLM Validation: Detailed analysis (ValidationReport)
4. Apply Result:
   - ACCEPT: Add to playbook as active (or established if strong evidence)
   - ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION: Add as draft or candidate
   - REJECT: Don't add, optionally log for future

## Configuration

validationEnabled: boolean (config, default true)
validationLookbackDays: number (config, default 90)
Evidence gate thresholds (configurable):
- minSessionsForActive: 5
- minSessionsForDraft: 2
- maxFailureRatio: 0.3 (30% failures still acceptable)

## Acceptance Criteria
- ValidationResult interface with all fields
- ValidationReport interface with comprehensive structure
- Evidence interface for evidence items
- EvidenceGateResult interface for pre-LLM gate
- Zod schemas for all types
- TypeScript exports
- Example validation reports showing ACCEPT/REJECT/CAUTION
- JSDoc documentation
- Integration with validation command","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:06.53211-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:26.623953-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y22j","title":"[Docs] Configuration reference documentation","description":"Document all configuration options with types, defaults, and examples.

Configuration sources (precedence order):
1. Command-line flags (highest)
2. Environment variables
3. Repo config (.cass/config.yaml)
4. Global config (~/.cass-memory/config.json)
5. Defaults (lowest)

Global config: llm_provider, default_model, confidence_threshold, decay_half_life_days, max_bullets_per_section
Repo config: project_name, playbook_path, diary_path, session_path, validation_rules
Environment variables: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CASS_MEMORY_CONFIG, CASS_MEMORY_DEBUG

Include JSON schema for validation and example configs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:28.411185-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:13.944052-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y5s","title":"[Curator] pruneDeprecated - Remove deprecated from active list","description":"Remove deprecated bullets from active list and move to deprecated section.

Signature:
function pruneDeprecated(playbook: Playbook): number

Process:
1. Initialize pruned counter
2. Filter playbook.bullets:
   - Keep bullets where maturity !== 'deprecated'
   - Collect deprecated bullets
3. For each deprecated bullet:
   - Add to playbook.deprecated array (if it exists)
   - Increment pruned counter
4. Update playbook.bullets to filtered array
5. Return pruned count

Important notes:
- Does NOT delete data
- Deprecated bullets move to separate section for history
- Maintains complete audit trail
- Allows future analysis of what didn't work

Why separate active from deprecated?
- Keeps active playbook focused and clean
- Prevents deprecated rules from being used
- Preserves history for learning
- Enables deprecation analytics

Playbook structure after pruning:
{
  bullets: [...active bullets...],
  deprecated: [...deprecated bullets...],
  stats: {
    totalActive: bullets.length,
    totalDeprecated: deprecated.length
  }
}

This is called at the END of curatePlaybook, after auto-inversion.

Prune order matters:
1. Process all deltas
2. Auto-invert harmful bullets (creates deprecations)
3. Prune deprecated (moves them out)
4. Return final playbook","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:06.4804-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:46.957599-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y6t","title":"[Types] CommandResult and Error Types","description":"## Overview
Define command result and error types for consistent CLI output and error handling across all cass-memory commands.

## Purpose
Standardize:
1. Success/failure responses from all commands
2. Error reporting with actionable messages
3. Data payloads for JSON output mode
4. Audit violation reporting
5. Health check results

## CommandResult

Standard return type for all CLI commands.

Fields:

success: boolean
- True if command completed successfully
- False if command failed
- Example: true

message: string
- Human-readable summary of result
- Success: 'Added 3 new rules to playbook'
- Failure: 'Failed to load playbook: file not found'
- Required: Always present

data: unknown | undefined
- Optional command-specific payload
- Type varies by command
- Examples:
  - context command: ContextResult
  - stats command: StatsReport
  - validate command: ValidationReport
- JSON mode: Serialized to output

error: string | undefined
- Optional error details for failures
- Stack trace (in verbose mode)
- Technical details
- Example: 'ENOENT: no such file or directory, open ~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml'

## AuditViolation

Individual violation found by audit command.

Fields:

bulletId: string
- ID of bullet that was violated or contradicted
- Example: 'b-abc123'

bulletContent: string
- Text of the rule that was violated
- Example: 'Use TokenValidator for JWT validation'

sessionPath: string
- Path to session where violation occurred
- Example: '/cass/2025-12-07-api-impl.jsonl'

evidence: string
- Excerpt showing the violation
- Example: 'Used direct JWT decode without TokenValidator'

severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'
- How serious the violation is
- low: Stylistic deviation
- medium: Best practice not followed
- high: Security/correctness issue

## AuditResult

Complete audit report from audit command.

Fields:

sessionsScanned: number
- Count of sessions analyzed
- Example: 25

violations: AuditViolation[]
- Array of found violations
- Grouped by: severity, then bulletId
- Example: [{ bulletId: 'b-abc', bulletContent: 'Use TokenValidator', sessionPath: '/cass/...', evidence: '...', severity: 'high' }]

suggestions: string[]
- Actionable recommendations
- Examples:
  - 'Consider deprecating rule b-xyz - violated in 80% of recent sessions'
  - 'Rule b-abc is consistently followed - promote to proven'
  - 'Add anti-pattern for direct JWT decode based on 5 violations'

## ErrorTypes

Common error categories for better error handling.

Categories:

ConfigError:
- Config file not found
- Invalid config format
- Missing required config fields

PlaybookError:
- Playbook file not found
- Invalid playbook YAML
- Schema validation failure

CassError:
- cass executable not found
- cass search failed
- cass timeout

ValidationError:
- Invalid bullet data
- Rule validation failed
- Evidence gate not passed

LLMError:
- API key missing
- API call failed
- Rate limit exceeded
- Invalid response format

## Success Response Examples

Context command:
{ success: true, message: 'Found 12 relevant rules for task', data: { task: '...', relevantBullets: [...], ... } }

Reflect command:
{ success: true, message: 'Processed 5 sessions, added 3 rules, updated 2', data: { addedCount: 3, updatedCount: 2 } }

Stats command:
{ success: true, message: 'Playbook statistics', data: { totalBullets: 42, activeCount: 35, ... } }

## Error Response Examples

Config error:
{ success: false, message: 'Config file not found', error: 'ENOENT: ~/.cass-memory/config.yaml' }

Playbook error:
{ success: false, message: 'Invalid playbook format', error: 'YAML parsing failed at line 42: unexpected indent' }

Validation error:
{ success: false, message: 'Validation failed', error: 'Insufficient evidence: only 1 session found' }

## JSON Output Mode

When config.jsonOutput = true:
- All commands return CommandResult as JSON
- Suppress human-friendly formatting
- Include full data payload
- Errors as structured objects

Example:
{ "success": true, "message": "Context retrieved", "data": { "task": "...", "relevantBullets": [...] } }

## Acceptance Criteria
- CommandResult interface with all fields
- AuditViolation interface
- AuditResult interface
- Error type definitions
- Success/error response examples
- JSON serialization support
- Zod schemas for validation
- TypeScript exports
- JSDoc documentation
- Consistent error messages across commands","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:08.437115-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:26.871514-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-y7x","title":"[Generator] separateAntiPatterns - Split rules from pitfalls","description":"Separate anti-patterns from regular rules for distinct presentation.

SIGNATURE:
function separateAntiPatterns(scoredBullets: ScoredBullet[]): { rules: ScoredBullet[]; antiPatterns: ScoredBullet[] }

PURPOSE:
Split scored bullets into two categories for different sections of the context output. Anti-patterns (pitfalls) are shown separately to highlight what NOT to do.

CLASSIFICATION LOGIC:
A bullet is an anti-pattern if:
  bullet.type === 'anti-pattern' || bullet.kind === 'anti_pattern'

Both field names supported for backwards compatibility.

OUTPUT STRUCTURE:
{
  rules: ScoredBullet[],        // Regular patterns/rules (what to do)
  antiPatterns: ScoredBullet[]  // Anti-patterns (what NOT to do)
}

PRESENTATION:
Rules appear in: 'Relevant Playbook Rules' section
Anti-patterns appear in: 'PITFALLS TO AVOID' section

EXAMPLE:
Input: [
  { id: 'b1', content: 'Use async/await for DB calls', type: 'pattern' },
  { id: 'b2', content: 'AVOID: Blocking DB calls in event loop', type: 'anti-pattern' },
  { id: 'b3', content: 'Cache frequently-read data', type: 'optimization' }
]

Output: {
  rules: [b1, b3],
  antiPatterns: [b2]
}

SCORE PRESERVATION:
Both arrays maintain the scoring metadata (relevanceScore, effectiveScore, finalScore) from scorePlaybookBullets for potential display.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Simple filter operation (no mutation)
- Preserves order within each category
- Empty antiPatterns array is valid (no pitfalls relevant)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.792421-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:21.171652-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y8o","title":"Phase 3: Advanced Features + Intelligence","description":"# EPIC: Advanced Features + Intelligence

## Purpose
Add intelligent features that dramatically improve relevance and usability:
local semantic search, multi-iteration reflection, scientific validation,
and playbook health analytics.

## Background & Reasoning
Simple keyword matching for bullet relevance achieves ~60% accuracy. By adding
local semantic embeddings via @xenova/transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 23MB model),
we can reach ~90% relevance without network calls.

The GPT Pro proposal introduced "scientific validation" - before accepting a new
rule, query cass for historical evidence. Did this pattern succeed or fail in the
past? This prevents accepting rules that contradict historical evidence.

Multi-iteration reflection (from ACE paper) runs the reflector multiple times per
session, focusing on different aspects each iteration. This catches insights that
a single pass might miss.

## Priority Features (from ROI analysis)
- P7: Local semantic search @xenova/transformers (4× impact)
- P9: Stats command playbook health dashboard (2× impact)

## Key Deliverables
1. `cass-memory validate "<rule>"` - Scientific validation against history
2. `cass-memory stats` - Playbook health dashboard
3. Local embedding-based relevance scoring
4. Multi-iteration reflector (configurable 1-3 iterations)
5. Cross-agent enrichment in diaries

## Technical Specifications
### Semantic Search
- Model: Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (runs locally, ~23MB)
- Embedding dimension: 384
- Scoring: 40% keyword + 60% semantic similarity
- Cache: ~/.cass-memory/embeddings/bullets.embeddings

### Stats Dashboard Metrics
- Total bullets by scope (global/workspace)
- Distribution by maturity (candidate/established/proven/retired)
- Distribution by kind (project_convention/stack_pattern/workflow_rule/anti_pattern)
- Top performers (highest effective scores)
- At-risk bullets (negative effective scores)
- Stale bullets (no feedback in 90+ days)
- Merge candidates (high semantic similarity pairs)

## Dependencies
- Requires Phase 2 completion (diary, reflect, curate pipeline)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:16:41.554097-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:16:41.554097-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-0ew","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:25.218629-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","title":"Validate Command: Scientific Validation (GPT Pro Pattern)","description":"# Feature: Validate Command (Scientific Validation)

## Purpose
Validate a proposed rule against historical evidence from cass.
This implements the GPT Pro proposal's "scientific validation" - 
treating rule proposals as hypotheses to be tested against data.

## Usage
```bash
cass-memory validate "Always use httpx instead of requests" --json
cass-memory validate "Prefer composition over inheritance" --verbose
```

## Process Flow
```
1. Extract validation keywords from proposed rule
2. Check evidence-count gate (auto-accept/reject if clear signal)
3. If ambiguous, run LLM validator with evidence
4. Return verdict with reasoning
```

## Implementation
```typescript
async function validateCommand(
  proposedRule: string,
  flags: ValidateFlags
): Promise<void> {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  
  // 1. Extract keywords
  const keywords = extractKeywords(proposedRule);
  
  // 2. Check evidence gate
  const gateResult = await evidenceCountGate(proposedRule, config);
  
  if (gateResult.action !== "needs-llm") {
    output({
      proposedRule,
      verdict: gateResult.action === "auto-accept" ? "ACCEPT" : "REJECT",
      confidence: gateResult.confidence,
      reason: `${gateResult.action}: ${gateResult.successCount} successes, ${gateResult.failureCount} failures`,
      evidence: gateResult.relevantSessions.map(h => ({
        session: h.source_path,
        snippet: h.snippet,
        outcome: classifySessionOutcome(h)
      }))
    }, formatValidationResult, flags);
    return;
  }
  
  // 3. Run LLM validator
  const validation = await runValidator(
    ValidationResultSchema,
    proposedRule,
    formatEvidenceForPrompt(gateResult.relevantSessions),
    config
  );
  
  output({
    proposedRule,
    verdict: validation.valid ? "ACCEPT" : "REJECT",
    confidence: validation.confidence || 0.7,
    reason: validation.reason,
    refinedRule: validation.refinedContent,
    evidence: gateResult.relevantSessions.map(h => ({
      session: h.source_path,
      snippet: h.snippet
    }))
  }, formatValidationResult, flags);
}
```

## Output Example
```json
{
  "proposedRule": "Always use httpx instead of requests",
  "verdict": "ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION",
  "confidence": 0.75,
  "reason": "Evidence shows httpx succeeded in 4 sessions for async use cases, but requests was fine for simple sync calls",
  "refinedRule": "Prefer httpx for async HTTP calls; requests is acceptable for simple synchronous requests",
  "evidence": [
    {
      "session": "~/.claude/sessions/abc.jsonl",
      "snippet": "Switched to httpx for async support, much cleaner code"
    },
    {
      "session": "~/.cursor/sessions/def.jsonl", 
      "snippet": "requests worked fine for this simple GET request"
    }
  ]
}
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:24:14.956313-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:24:14.956313-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:24:14.958057-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-0ew.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:25.294018-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:25.373799-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.2","title":"Stats Command: Playbook Health Dashboard (P9)","description":"# Feature: Stats Command (P9 - Playbook Health Dashboard)

## Purpose
Display comprehensive playbook health metrics. Helps identify:
- At-risk bullets that need attention
- Stale bullets that may be outdated
- Top performers worth promoting
- Merge candidates (similar content)

## Usage
```bash
cass-memory stats
cass-memory stats --json
cass-memory stats --category testing
```

## Metrics Displayed

### 1. Overview
- Total bullets (global vs workspace)
- Distribution by state (draft/active/proven/retired)
- Distribution by kind (project_convention/stack_pattern/workflow_rule/anti_pattern)

### 2. Score Distribution
- Excellent (effective score > 10)
- Good (5-10)
- Neutral (0-5)
- At Risk (< 0)

### 3. Top Performers
- Top 5 bullets by effective score
- Most frequently marked helpful

### 4. Needs Attention
- Bullets with negative effective scores
- Stale bullets (no feedback in 90+ days)
- Merge candidates (similarity > 0.8)

## Implementation
```typescript
async function statsCommand(flags: StatsFlags): Promise<void> {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  const playbook = await loadMergedPlaybook(config);
  
  // Calculate all metrics
  const stats = {
    total: playbook.bullets.length,
    byScope: countByScope(playbook.bullets),
    byState: countByState(playbook.bullets),
    byKind: countByKind(playbook.bullets),
    scoreDistribution: calculateScoreDistribution(playbook.bullets, config),
    topPerformers: getTopPerformers(playbook.bullets, config, 5),
    atRisk: getAtRiskBullets(playbook.bullets, config),
    stale: getStaleBullets(playbook.bullets, 90),
    mergeCandidates: findMergeCandidates(playbook.bullets, 0.8)
  };
  
  output(stats, formatStatsHuman, flags);
}
```

## Human Output
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│              CASS-MEMORY PLAYBOOK HEALTH                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Total Bullets: 156 (Global: 89, Workspace: 67)              │
│                                                             │
│ BY STATE:                                                   │
│   ● Draft:       23 (14.7%)  ░░░░░                          │
│   ● Active:      87 (55.8%)  ████████████████               │
│   ● Proven:      34 (21.8%)  ███████                        │
│   ● Retired:     12 (7.7%)   ███                            │
│                                                             │
│ BY KIND:                                                    │
│   project_convention: 42  stack_pattern: 58                 │
│   workflow_rule: 48       anti_pattern: 8                   │
│                                                             │
│ SCORE DISTRIBUTION:                                         │
│   Excellent (>10):  18  ████                                │
│   Good (5-10):      45  ██████████                          │
│   Neutral (0-5):    71  ████████████████                    │
│   At Risk (<0):     10  ███  ⚠️                             │
│                                                             │
│ TOP PERFORMERS:                                             │
│   1. b-abc123 (24.3) "Use waitFor for async assertions..."  │
│   2. b-def456 (18.7) "Prefer httpx for async HTTP..."       │
│   3. b-ghi789 (15.2) "Mock external APIs in tests..."       │
│                                                             │
│ NEEDS ATTENTION:                                            │
│   ⚠️ 10 bullets at risk (negative scores)                   │
│   🕐 8 bullets stale (no feedback 90+ days)                 │
